core_mechanics: Players control a 2D vehicle (composed of a main body and two wheels) using keyboard inputs to accelerate and brake. The game environment is a procedurally generated undulating terrain using Matter.js static bodies. Vehicle movement is governed by Matter.js physics, including gravity, friction, and applied forces/torques to the wheels. Fuel constantly depletes and must be replenished by collecting fuel canisters that are dynamically placed on the terrain. The objective is to travel a set distance per level without running out of fuel or crashing. Stunts, specifically full front or back flips, award bonus points. The game tracks current score, distance traveled, fuel level, and the current level progress.
visual_elements:
• Player Vehicle: Rendered as a combination of simple p5.js shapes: a dark grey rectangle or custom polygon for the main chassis body, and two black circles for the wheels. Wheels should rotate visually with vehicle movement.
  • Terrain: Rendered as a series of connected brown or dark green Matter.js static bodies (rectangles/polygons), appearing as a continuous, undulating ground. The ground should be filled below the surface for visual depth.
  • Fuel Canister: Rendered as a small, bright green cylinder or rectangle, dynamically positioned on the terrain. It should visually disappear upon collision with the vehicle.
  • Background: A simple blue-to-light blue gradient for the sky.
  • User Interface (UI):
    • Score Display: White text, 'Score: [Current Score]', positioned in the top-left corner.
    • High Score Display: White text, 'High Score: [Best Score]', displayed on the start menu and game over screen.
    • Fuel Gauge: A horizontal green bar positioned in the top-center, decreasing in width as fuel depletes.
    • Distance Traveled: White text, 'Distance: [Current Distance]m', positioned below the fuel gauge or top-right.
    • Level Indicator: White text, 'Level: [Current Level Number]', positioned in the top-right corner.
    • Game State Overlays: 'Press Any Key to Start', 'PAUSED', 'LEVEL COMPLETE!', 'GAME OVER!', 'YOU WIN!' messages, centrally displayed with clear, large white text.
  • Visual Effects:
    • Wheel Rotation: Wheels visibly spin.
    • Engine Exhaust: Small, light gray particle effect emanating from the rear of the vehicle when accelerating.
    • Fuel Collection: A quick, small 'poof' or 'starburst' animation when a fuel canister is collected.
    • Crash/Explosion: A temporary expanding red-orange circle animation on vehicle destruction (e.g., out of fuel, prolonged upside down).
    • Screen Shake: Brief, subtle screen shake on hard landings or significant impacts.
control_scheme:
• ArrowRight (or D): Accelerate. Applies forward torque or force to the vehicle's rear wheel, causing the vehicle to move right.
  • ArrowLeft (or A): Brake / Reverse. Applies reverse torque or force to the vehicle's rear wheel. If the vehicle is moving forward, it acts as a brake. If the vehicle is stationary or moving backward, it causes the vehicle to reverse.
  • Space: (In Menu States) Select / Start Game / Continue.
game_states:
• START_MENU: The initial state. Displays the game title, a 'Press Space to Start' prompt, and the current high score.
    • Transition: Pressing 'Space' key → PLAYING (starts Level 1).
  • PLAYING: The active game state where the player controls the vehicle, collects fuel, and navigates terrain.
    • Transition:
      • Fuel runs out, vehicle flips/gets stuck, or falls off-screen → GAME_OVER.
      • Player reaches level-specific distance goal → LEVEL_COMPLETE.
  • LEVEL_COMPLETE: Entered upon successfully reaching the distance goal for the current level. Displays 'LEVEL [X] COMPLETE!' and the score for that level.
    • Transition: After a short delay (e.g., 3 seconds) or pressing 'Space' → PLAYING (advances to the next level if available) or WIN (if all levels completed).
  • GAME_OVER: Entered when the player fails a level (runs out of fuel, crashes, etc.). Displays 'GAME OVER!', the final score, and 'Press Space to Restart'.
    • Transition: Pressing 'Space' key → START_MENU.
  • WIN: Entered after successfully completing the final level. Displays 'YOU WIN!', the total final score, and 'Press Space to Play Again'.
    • Transition: Pressing 'Space' key → START_MENU.
win_conditions: The player successfully navigates and reaches the predetermined distance goal for all defined levels, culminating in the completion of the final level's track segment.
lose_conditions:
• Fuel Depletion: The vehicle's fuel level drops to zero.
  • Vehicle Stalled/Stuck: The vehicle's main body comes to a complete halt (velocity near zero) for more than 3 consecutive seconds, indicating it's stuck on terrain.
  • Vehicle Inverted: The vehicle's main body remains flipped upside down (rotated beyond +/- 90 degrees from horizontal) for more than 2 consecutive seconds.
  • Off-Screen: The vehicle's primary body falls too far below the visible screen area (e.g., below -500px Y-coordinate relative to camera).
scoring_system:
• Distance Traveled: +1 point per meter traveled.
  • Fuel Canister Collected: +50 points per canister.
  • Front Flip (360°): +100 points for a full forward rotation of the vehicle body.
  • Back Flip (360°): +100 points for a full backward rotation of the vehicle body.
  • Air Time: +1 point per 0.1 seconds the vehicle's body is more than 10 pixels above any terrain surface.
  • Level Completion Bonus: +500 points upon successfully completing any level.

Display: 'Score: [Current Score]' will be prominently displayed in the top-left corner during the PLAYING state. 'High Score: [Best Score]' will be displayed on the START_MENU and GAME_OVER screens. Scores do not directly affect gameplay but are for player achievement and high score tracking.

level_system.name: Rolling Hills
level_system.description: Gentle, rolling hills with long, flat sections suitable for new players. Abundant fuel canisters ensure a forgiving experience.

level_system.name: Rocky Ascent
level_system.description: Steeper inclines and declines, interspersed with more frequent bumps and short drops. Requires more precise throttle control. Fuel is less frequent.

difficulty_factors.terrain_complexity: More varied, with occasional jagged edges and small jumps.

level_system.name: Mountain Pass
level_system.description: Challenging terrain with very steep climbs, significant drops, and sharp transitions. Mastering air control and careful throttle management is crucial. Fuel is scarce.

difficulty_factors.terrain_complexity: Extreme undulations, large gaps, and demanding landing zones.

simplifications:
• Single Player Only: All multiplayer modes, competitive cups, weekly events, and league progression are removed.
  • No Track Editor: The custom track editor feature is omitted.
  • Simplified Vehicle Progression: The concept of unlocking multiple unique vehicles and upgrading specific parts (engine, suspension, tires, 4WD) up to 20 levels each is removed. The game will feature a single, fixed vehicle with base physics properties.
  • Abstracted Economy: No coins, gems, or in-game store for purchases/upgrades.
  • Basic Visuals: Simple shapes and colors for all game objects, without detailed textures or complex animations.
  • Limited Stunts: Only front and back flips are recognized for bonus points; other complex stunt variations are ignored.
  • Basic Procedural Generation: Terrain generation will rely on simpler algorithms (e.g., Perlin noise or basic trigonometric functions) to create undulating hills, rather than complex biome systems.
implementation_notes:
• Physics Engine: Matter.js will be used for all rigid body physics simulation, including the vehicle, wheels, and terrain.
  • Rendering: p5.js will be used for all drawing operations, including shapes, text, and visual effects, utilizing Matter.js body positions and rotations.
  • Camera System: The p5.js canvas should implement a camera that follows the player vehicle, possibly with a slight damping or lead to anticipate movement. Terrain should be rendered relative to the camera position.
  • Dynamic Terrain: As the player advances, new terrain segments and fuel canisters should be procedurally generated and added to the Matter.js world. Off-screen, far-behind terrain segments should be removed to maintain performance.
  • Fuel System Logic: A global variable will track fuel. It decreases by a small amount per frame and is increased when the vehicle collides with a fuel canister.
  • Score & Level Display: The current score, fuel gauge, distance, and current level number must be continuously displayed on screen during the PLAYING state as specified.
  • Level Advancement: Upon reaching the 'track_length_meters' for the current level, the game will transition to the LEVEL_COMPLETE state, then load the next level with its defined difficulty parameters.
  • High Score: The highest score achieved will be stored locally using `localStorage` to persist across game sessions.
